Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 264. The Akitu Chapel of Isana (CT 53 214)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

ša URU.i-⸢sa⸣-[na]

o 2'2'

ap-ti-qi-[di]

(2) I have appointed [......] of Isana. All is well, nobody is missing. 30 courses of bricks of the akitu chapel were laid on the 3rd.

o 3'3'

DI-mu a—dan-[niš]

o 4'4'

01-en la i*-⸢ma⸣-[ṭi]

o 5'5'

30 ti-⸢ik⸣-[pi]

o 6'6'

ša É.a-⸢ki⸣-[ti]

Bottom
b.e.b.e. uninscribed

Reverse
r 1r 1

UD 03-KÁM ⸢kar⸣-[ru]

r 22

LUGAL be-lí ⸢ú⸣-[da]

(r 2) The king my lord knows that I have no grain stores. I would like to appoint my major-domo with [my] (other) offici[als] so I could go and [...] grain stores [...

r 33

ka-ru-a-ni-ia ⸢la⸣-[áš-šú]

r 44

LÚv.GAL—É-⸢ia⸣

r 55

TAv LÚv.EN—pi-qi-⸢ta⸣-[ti]

r 66

la-ap-qi-⸢di⸣

r 77

⸢a⸣-na-ku la-al-[lik]

r 88

⸢ka⸣-ru-⸢a⸣-[ni]

r 99

[x x] ⸢x⸣ [x x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P313629/.
